exercises.title: Trace basic CF chain
hints: supplyAsync: 6
thenApply: 6 * 7 = 42
thenApply: 'Answer: 42'
thenAccept: prints it

exercises.title: supplyAsync and thenApply
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to create an async uppercase transformer.
hints: supplyAsync takes Supplier<T>
thenApply takes Function<T, R>

exercises.title: exceptionally for fallback
exercises.scenario: Handle async failure with a default value.
exercises.instructions:
Create a CF that simulates a failing network call. Use exceptionally to return 'default data' on failure.

Expected output:
Result: default data
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// CF with exceptionally fallback
hints: supplyAsync throws RuntimeException
exceptionally catches it and returns the fallback
No need to catch at get() — exceptionally handled it

exercises.title: thenAccept and thenRun
exercises.scenario: Terminal operations that consume or ignore the result.
exercises.instructions:
Chain a CF: compute sum 1-10 → thenAccept prints 'Sum: N' → thenRun prints 'Done'.

Expected output:
Sum: 55
Done
hints: thenAccept(Consumer) gets the result (55) and prints
thenRun(Runnable) runs after, no access to result
get() on the final CF waits for completion

exercises.title: allOf — wait for all three
exercises.scenario: Fan-out to three concurrent tasks, wait for all.
exercises.instructions:
Submit 3 async tasks (200ms, 100ms, 300ms). Use allOf to wait for all, then join() each and print results.

Expected output (order fixed by join order):
A: Result-A
B: Result-B
C: Result-C
Total time: ~300ms
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// allOf: wait for all three CFs
hints: All three CFs start simultaneously — not sequential
allOf().thenRun: callback runs when all three complete
join() inside thenRun doesn't block — CFs already done

exercises.title: thenCompose — sequential async steps
exercises.scenario: Chain dependent async operations without nesting.
exercises.instructions:
Simulate: fetch user (200ms) → fetch orders for that user (150ms) using thenCompose. Print user and order count.

Expected output:
User: Aisha
Orders for Aisha: 5
Total time: ~350ms
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// thenCompose for sequential async steps
hints: thenCompose: user → CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(fetch orders)
Total: 200+150 = 350ms (sequential steps)
thenApply here would give CF<CF<String>> — wrong

exercises.title: anyOf — fastest service wins
exercises.scenario: First successful result from redundant async sources.
exercises.instructions:
Submit 3 tasks with different delays (500ms, 200ms, 350ms). anyOf returns the first. Print which one won and total time.

Expected output:
Fastest: Server-B (200ms)
Time: ~200ms
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// anyOf: fastest service wins
hints: anyOf completes when ANY one completes
Server-B at 200ms is fastest
Other CFs still run in the background

exercises.title: handle — both success and failure
exercises.scenario: handle runs for both success and error paths.
exercises.instructions:
Create two CFs: one successful, one failing. Use handle on each to produce a safe result string.

Expected output:
Success handled: VALUE=42
Error handled: FALLBACK (division by zero)
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// handle: runs on both success and failure

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Success handled: VALUE=42
Error handled: FALLBACK (/ by zero)

hints: handle(BiFunction<T, Throwable, R>): one or other is non-null
On success: ex is null, val has the result
On failure: ex is the CompletionException (getCause for original)

exercises.title: allOf with stream of CFs
exercises.scenario: Create many CFs from a list, collect with allOf.
exercises.instructions:
Fetch stock prices for 5 symbols concurrently using CompletableFuture. Collect all with allOf, print sorted by price.

Stocks and prices: TCS:3890, RELIANCE:2450, INFY:1560, HDFC:1678, WIPRO:412

Expected output:
TCS: ₹3890
RELIANCE: ₹2450
HDFC: ₹1678
INFY: ₹1560
WIPRO: ₹412
hints: Create list of CFs from stream.map
allOf(futures.toArray(new CompletableFuture[0]))
thenRun: join each (already done), sort, print

exercises.title: CF with custom executor — thread names
exercises.scenario: Custom executor controls which thread runs each stage.
hints: supplyAsync runs in the pool thread
Pool thread is named 'pool-thread'
thenApply prepends 'was: '

exercises.title: Full payment pipeline with thenCompose
exercises.scenario: Three sequential async steps, each dependent on previous.
exercises.instructions:
Build a payment pipeline: validateCard(200ms) → authorizePayment(150ms) → updateLedger(100ms). Use thenCompose for each step. Handle failures at any step with exceptionally.

Expected output:
Payment pipeline:
  Validated: card-4242
  Authorized: ₹1500 on card-4242
  Ledger updated: TXN-001
Payment successful!
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Sequential payment pipeline with thenCompose

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Payment pipeline:
  Validated: card-4242
  Authorized: ₹1500 on card-4242
  Ledger updated: TXN-001
Payment successful!

hints: Each step takes result of previous → use thenCompose
exceptionally at the end catches failures from ANY step in the chain
Total time: 200+150+100 = 450ms sequential (but no blocking threads)

exercises.title: Retry with CompletableFuture
exercises.instructions:
Build a retry mechanism: an operation fails 2 times then succeeds on the 3rd attempt. Use CompletableFuture and recursion to retry up to 3 times.

Expected output:
Attempt 1: failed
Attempt 2: failed
Attempt 3: succeeded
Final result: Data on attempt 3

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Attempt 1: failed
Attempt 2: failed
Attempt 3: succeeded
Final result: Data on attempt 3

hints: exceptionally: if retries remain, call fetchWithRetry recursively
join() inside exceptionally — acceptable since it's a retry pattern
AtomicInteger tracks attempt count across async calls

exercises.title: Product page aggregator — full fan-out
exercises.scenario: Fan-out to 3 services, combine results, timeout fallback.
exercises.instructions:
Build the worked-example product page loader:
- fetchProduct(id): 200ms → "Wireless Mouse | ₹799"
- fetchRating(id): 150ms → 4.3
- checkInventory(id): 100ms → true

All three run in parallel. allOf combines. Handle errors with exceptionally.

Expected output:
=== Product Page ===
Product: Wireless Mouse | ₹799
Rating:  4.3 ★
Status:  In Stock
Loaded in ~200ms
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// Product page: fan-out to 3 services
hints: All three CFs start immediately — parallel, not sequential
allOf().thenRun: join each (already done)
Total time ~200ms (slowest service), not 450ms

exercises.title: whenComplete for side effects + logging
exercises.scenario: Log every CF completion regardless of success or failure.
exercises.instructions:
Build an async service with logging: every operation logs its outcome via whenComplete. Run 3 operations: add (success), divide (failure), multiply (success).

Expected output:
[LOG] add completed: result=15
[LOG] divide failed: / by zero
[LOG] multiply completed: result=42
Final results: 15, -1 (error), 42
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.concurrent.*;
// whenComplete for audit logging
hints: whenComplete: runs on both success and failure, doesn't change result
ex is CompletionException — getCause() for original
divide.exceptionally(e -> -1) provides fallback for the final collection

exercises.title: Complete async order system — Module 10 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full async order processing combining all Module 10 concurrency concepts.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete async order processor using everything from Module 10:
- 3 orders processed concurrently (ExecutorService + CompletableFuture)
- Each order: validate → price → confirm (sequential, thenCompose)
- Results collected via allOf
- Failures handled with exceptionally
- AtomicInteger counts successful orders
- ConcurrentHashMap tracks order statuses

Expected output:
Processing 3 orders...
ORD-001: CONFIRMED ₹1500
ORD-002: FAILED invalid amount
ORD-003: CONFIRMED ₹3000
Successful: 2/3
Order statuses: {ORD-001=CONFIRMED, ORD-002=FAILED, ORD-003=CONFIRMED}
hints: processOrder: 3 sequential thenCompose steps
whenComplete updates ConcurrentHashMap and AtomicInteger
exceptionally at end catches validation failure
allOf waits for all; then join each in order
